content.body:
Sorry I missed this before.

Do you have a reproduction of a perf issue for this? Is the concern around style invalidation?
(You also have to ensure `:heading` is as optimized as a type selector is.)
content.formatted_body: (You also have to ensure :heading is as optimized as a type selector is.)

content.body: So a headingoffset changes and you walk the tree and invalidate prior offsets? Why do you need to walk up and down?
keithamus: if an element gets replotted, that might impact an arbitrary descendant heading element, but in order to compute the offset for that heading element you have to go up
content.formatted_body: keithamus: if an element gets replotted, that might impact an arbitrary descendant heading element, but in order to compute the offset for that heading element you have to go up

content.body: oh yeah. To the first `headingreset` or root, I guess. Does this actually cause perf issues though?
content.formatted_body: oh yeah. To the first headingreset or root, I guess. Does this actually cause perf issues though?

content.body: keithamus: I suspect it will cause issues on websites as large as GitHub that are already prone to perf issues
content.formatted_body: keithamus: I suspect it will cause issues on websites as large as GitHub that are already prone to perf issues

content.body: I suppose one could keep a flag on trees as to whether or not they have a reset, to avoid a walk for nothing
There's quite a few optimizations possible of course, but the question I'm having is whether this is too complexity for the amount of benefit we're getting.
